title: Floyd's tortoise-and-hare: cycle detection in O(1) memory
statement_informal: Floyd detects a cycle in the orbit x, f(x), f(f(x)), ... with two pointers (one twice as fast) and O(1) memory. Whenever the orbit repeats a value (guaranteed by pigeonhole for finite reachable sets), there is a step m>=1 at which tortoise (m steps) and hare (2m steps) coincide. No visited set is stored.

cost_model.notes: Two pointers, O(1) space. From a collision iter f i x = iter f j x (i<j), the orbit is periodic with period j-i from index i; choosing m = (i+1)(j-i) (a multiple of the period, >= i) gives iter f m x = iter f (2m) x.

references: R. W. Floyd, cycle-finding ('tortoise and hare'); Knuth TAOCP Vol. 2 §3.1 ex. 6 discusses the same periodicity (mu, lambda).
A collision is guaranteed by pigeonhole whenever the reachable set is finite.
